title: Report Writer
learning_objectives: Write professional vulnerability reports following industry standards
Document proof-of-concept with clear reproduction steps
scenario: During a lab exercise, you discovered a simulated vulnerability in the practice web application. Write a professional vulnerability report as if submitting to a real bug bounty programme. This is about communication, not exploitation.

scenario_context.relevance: The report is the primary deliverable of a penetration test. A technically brilliant finding with a poor report has no value.

subtasks.title: Vulnerability Documentation
subtasks.description: Write a complete report following the template format: title, summary, impact, steps to reproduce, severity, remediation.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Title should be descriptive. Summary in one paragraph. Impact in business terms. CVSS score with justification. Steps to reproduce must be detailed enough for someone else to follow.

subtasks.title: Proof of Concept
subtasks.description: Create clear PoC documentation with step-by-step reproduction instructions and annotated screenshots.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Every step should be numbered. Include exact URLs, payloads, and expected results. Annotate screenshots to highlight the vulnerability. Anyone should be able to reproduce from your instructions.

subtasks.title: Remediation Recommendation
subtasks.description: Research and recommend specific remediation steps with references to security best practices.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Go beyond 'fix the vulnerability'. Recommend specific code changes, configurations, or architectural improvements. Reference OWASP, CWE, or vendor documentation.

reflection_prompt: You find a vulnerability that allows access to 100,000 customer records. The company's bug bounty programme offers $500. Do you report it? What ethical considerations guide your decision?

quiz.question: The most important part of a vulnerability report is:
options: Clear, reproducible steps that allow anyone to verify the finding
quiz.explanation: If a finding can't be reproduced, it can't be verified or fixed. Clear reproduction steps are the foundation of a useful report.

quiz.question: Remediation recommendations should:
options: Provide specific, actionable steps with references to best practices
quiz.explanation: Specific, referenced remediation guidance helps clients fix issues quickly and correctly, adding value to the engagement.

mentor_notes: Report quality is the deliverable. A well-written report for a simple finding scores higher than a poorly documented critical finding. Check that PoC steps are reproducible.
